notes: Spec asserts the auto-executed tool-call arc: ready → working → ready with a tool call mid-turn, and the invariant that state NEVER enters waiting. The recipe drives a single auto-approved shell call ('echo hello') under `kiro-cli chat --trust-all-tools` followed by a terminal text-only reply. The bash_tool_use phase is the mid-turn tool-use marker (kirocli/parser.go: AssistantMessage with toolUse → assistant+ToolUses opens the tool; ToolResults closes via ToolResultIDs) that anchors the no-waiting invariant on the working span. --trust-all-tools means no approval picker, so PermissionPending is never set and waiting is unreachable; if the recording ever flips to waiting, the tool tripped a gate (different feature: tool-gate) and the recipe must be tightened. No cost/token assertions: the kiro-cli JSONL carries no model/token/cost (the sidecar holds credits/model, the daemon does not read it), so the verify observes model unknown, cost/tokens false — orthogonal to this state+tool-call scenario.

text: Session appears in ready (pre-session proc-<PID> row; kiro writes the .jsonl lazily on the first prompt)
Mid-turn AssistantMessage carries a toolUse block; the shell call auto-executes (--trust-all-tools, no approval); session stays working
Turn completes back to ready at the final text-only AssistantMessage; the tool ran auto-approved and state never entered waiting
